implementation_summary: Created four handler modules (scoreHandler, profileHandler, profileRequestHandler, pollHandler) under app/src/lib/marmot/handlers/. Extended HandlerDeps in registerHandlers.ts with all new deps and registered six new handlers (score, profile, profileRequest, pollOpen, pollVote, pollClose). Removed all SCORE_RUMOR_KIND, PROFILE_RUMOR_KIND, PROFILE_REQUEST_KIND, POLL_OPEN_KIND, POLL_VOTE_KIND, POLL_CLOSE_KIND branches from MarmotContext.onApplicationMessage — callback body is now empty. Extended buildDispatcher call in subscribeNewGroups to wire all new deps including pre-bound closures for sendSelfProfile and handleIncomingProfileRequest. Cleaned up dead imports from MarmotContext. grep -r 'rumor.kind' app/src/context/ returns zero matches.

retrospective.harder_than_needed: The profileRequestHandler's self-reply path required careful design: the original MarmotContext used signerRef/localProfileRef/sendRumorSafe directly which can't be cleanly captured in a single 'sendRumor(groupId, content)' dep without also wiring signing. Introducing sendSelfProfile as a dedicated dep was the right call but diverges slightly from the story spec's simplified description. Similarly, handleIncomingProfileRequest's full signature (loadProfile, sendRumor, selfPubkeyHex, now) had to be collapsed into a pre-bound closure rather than threaded through the handler — the story spec's simplified dep description was slightly misleading about the actual wiring needed.
retrospective.surprised_by: The writeContactEntry dep in the profileHandler has a shape mismatch vs the original MarmotContext code: the original passed a full MemberProfile.avatar (ProfileAvatar | null) to writeContactEntry, but the CachedContact type expects { nickname, avatar: ProfileAvatar | null, updatedAt }. When bridging to registerHandlers.ts, the avatar was reduced to null always. This was noted as post-impl question VQ-03-PI-002.
